_comment: OWASP SBOM-VEX-Taint-Analysis Benchmark Record — Layer 1: CycloneDX VEX + Layer 2: Annotation

analysis.detail: aalto-xml is declared optional in spring-web.gradle but is actively invoked at runtime by XmlEventDecoder.java when present on the classpath. XmlEventDecoder uses ClassUtils.isPresent() to detect aalto-xml and explicitly selects the Aalto-based decoding path via AaltoDataBufferToXmlEvent. Applications using Spring WebFlux with XML decoding that have aalto-xml on the classpath are exposed to the DoS vulnerability when processing untrusted XML input from network sources.
analysis.workaround: Exclude aalto-xml from the runtime classpath if XML decoding via XmlEventDecoder is not required. If XML decoding is required, ensure input is validated or sourced only from trusted parties.

cve.description: Malformed XML input triggers denial of service in aalto-xml parser

scope_signal.evidence: optional("com.fasterxml:aalto-xml") // out of order to avoid XML parser override
scope_signal.interpretation: Declared optional — not transitively inherited by consuming applications. Only present at runtime if explicitly added by downstream consumer.
scope_signal.note: This signal alone would suggest not_affected. It is a red herring without deeper analysis.

runtime_signal_initial.interpretation: Spring's internal XML loading for bean definitions uses JAXP, not aalto-xml. The in-code comment explicitly scopes this to application configuration files and notes XXE requires write access to config — not a network attack surface.
runtime_signal_initial.note: Second red herring. A pipeline that stops here will produce a wrong verdict.

evidence_lines: private static final boolean AALTO_PRESENT = ClassUtils.isPresent("com.fasterxml.aalto.AsyncXMLStreamReader", XmlEventDecoder.class.getClassLoader());
// when useAalto is true, decode() uses AaltoDataBufferToXmlEvent
code_path_signal.interpretation: Spring WebFlux's XmlEventDecoder explicitly detects aalto-xml at runtime and actively selects the Aalto-based decoding path when present. This is not passive classpath presence — it is a deliberate runtime preference for aalto-xml over alternatives.
code_path_signal.note: This is the decisive finding. Requires source code analysis beyond SBOM inspection.

conditions_for_exploitability: aalto-xml is present on the runtime classpath (explicitly added by consumer or transitive pull)
Application uses Spring WebFlux (reactive stack) with XML media type support
XmlEventDecoder is active in the codec pipeline
Application accepts XML input from untrusted network sources
context_signal.conditions_assessment: All four conditions are realistic and co-occur in Spring WebFlux applications doing XML REST APIs. Attack complexity is Low per CVSS — no special configuration required beyond classpath presence.
context_signal.note: Cannot determine from SBOM or Spring source alone whether a specific consuming application satisfies all conditions. Consumer-context dependent.

red_herrings.why_misleading: Optional means not transitively inherited, but does not mean never present at runtime. Consumer can and does explicitly add it.
red_herrings.pipeline_risk: A pipeline that stops at dependency scope will produce wrong verdict

red_herrings.why_misleading: Spring has multiple XML processing paths. JAXP path is for bean definition loading, not reactive HTTP codec processing.
red_herrings.pipeline_risk: A pipeline that generalizes from one XML path to all XML paths will produce wrong verdict

benchmark_annotation.context_note: Verdict is affected for Spring Framework as a library when aalto-xml is on the runtime classpath. The CVSS Low attack complexity confirms exploitation requires no special conditions beyond classpath presence and XML input acceptance.
benchmark_annotation.hitl_reason: CVSS score 7.5 exceeds pipeline threshold of 7.0. Human reviewer must approve before VEX is signed.
analysis_steps: Repository-wide source search for aalto-xml invocations (deterministic)
Exploitability condition assessment against CVSS metrics (semantic reasoning)

benchmark_metadata.difficulty_reason: Two sequential red herrings before decisive finding. Requires multi-file source analysis beyond SBOM. Tests pipeline depth.
interesting_because: Optional dependency that is actively preferred at runtime when present
Multiple XML processing paths in same project with different parsers
Correct verdict requires source code search, not just SBOM or build file inspection
Demonstrates insufficiency of SBOM-only VEX analysis
benchmark_metadata.key_lesson: SBOM-level analysis alone is insufficient for optional dependencies that are actively selected at runtime. Source code analysis is required.
